original_abstract: Exposure to air pollution represents a critical risk factor for a wide range of life-threatening diseases, making the development of robust forecasting systems essential for public health. Such mechanisms enable authorities to implement preemptive measures when elevated pollutant concentrations are anticipated. In recent years, Machine Learning—and specifically Deep Learning—has emerged as a dominant approach for predicting air quality. This paper provides a comprehensive systematic review of the primary contributions to this field between 2011 and 2021. Following a rigorous selection process across major scientific databases, 155 relevant studies were identified and analyzed. These papers are categorized and discussed based on their geographical focus, target pollutants, predictor variables, evaluation metrics, and the specific Machine Learning architectures employed.
humanized_abstract: Air pollution exposure is identified as one of the major risk factors for a vast array of fatal diseases and thus accurate air quality forecasting is needed to facilitate timely preventive actions. Therefore, the provision of reliable air quality forecasting systems is crucial for the protection of human health. In recent years, Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) techniques have been widely adopted for air quality prediction. This paper delivers a thorough systematic review of the major works published on air quality prediction using ML and DL from 2011 to 2021. Through an in-depth search of the major scientific literature databases, 155 research papers were identified and critically assessed. The extracted papers are systematically classified, examined and presented in relation to the location, the pollutants to be predicted, input features, performance evaluation criteria and ML techniques employed.
